title: Angel order in EVA Proposal
wikitext:
[]: Angel order in EVA Proposal             Quote Post by CL05114NERV » Sat Feb 13, 2010 7:22 pm Hey, for those who want to tackle this difficult task, I'm trying to come up with a reasonable list of the order of Angel's (or in the proposal's case, "Apostolos") that we would have been seen if the series went literally in the path of the proposal, and here is what I came up with so far 1. Adam 2. Lilith 3. Raziel 4. Shamshel 5. Ramiel 6. Gaghiel 7. Israfel 8. Sandalphon 9. Turel 10. Zeruel 11. Leliel 12. Bardiel 13. Arael 14. Sachiel 15. Tabris 16. Sahaquiel 17. Ireul 18. Matarael 19. Armisael 20. Shateiel 21. ? 22. ? 23. ? 24. ? 25. ? 26. ? 27. ? note the lack of the final 7 angels... which I know can't really be helped since there is a lack of information as far as I know of for the other Angel's mentioned in the proposal. Also, I somehow feel as though I misread part of the proposal and got some of the angel's out of order. So if you have any feelings that the list could be different, send me up your opinion! btw, if this topic is in the wrong place, warn me now so I may place it in the right one.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Sat Feb 13, 2010 7:27 pm Unless I'm mistaken wasn't Sachiel considered right after Raziel? As for the missing seven chances are two of them would have been modeled after the Unnamed and the Insubstantial. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Extracanonical_Mecha And maybe another two would have been modeled after Iblis and Barakijal, but I know nothing about The Nerv White Paper so.......

[]:             Quote Post by CL05114NERV » Sat Feb 13, 2010 7:58 pm I listed Sachiel as the angel right before Tabris because in the proposal it states right before his introduction, the episode before it will be an undersea battle around a sunken ship, and being that after Raziel it describes the same episode that happened when Shamsel attacked, I figured Shamshel had fit best right before Tabris

[]: Re: Angel order in EVA Proposal             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Feb 13, 2010 7:59 pm CL05114NERV wrote: 4. Shamshel

[]:             Quote Post by CL05114NERV » Sat Feb 13, 2010 8:09 pm makes sense, the only reason why I said Shamshel as the 4th angel was because of the fact in the proposal it said "Episode 3: The first telephone call: Shinji's new school. His first friends there. A heavy battle in Tokyo-3." the numbers I provided refer not to the proposal episode but rather the conventional way of numbering them in the order they were discovered first (Adam and Lilith being found before the series began and so on and so on)... thats why I tried to be careful with the interpretation... but your list makes sense as well. Oh yeah, and I forgot to add that Arael is described in the proposal as having some kind of swords in its wings and being capable of aerial combat as Reichu herself translated in the proposal "Hovering at high altitudes, the Apostolo Arael (Angel of Birds) A bird-shaped aerial combat weapon. With its twelve translucent wings outstretched, it can move freely about in the air. The Apostolo’s greatest weapons are the high-frequency swords on its wing-tips. Since the Evas are terrestrial units, they are forced into an extremely tough fight."

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Sun Feb 14, 2010 12:33 am The proposal says there will be 27 Angels total awakening *in addition* to Adam. It says 28 total including him. And the last 12 come from the moon, so how many do you have left not counting Adam and the Moon Angels? 15, same as in the series not counting Adam and Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by CL05114NERV » Sun Feb 14, 2010 2:25 am JoeD80 wrote: The proposal says there will be 27 Angels total awakening *in addition* to Adam. It says 28 total including him. And the last 12 come from the moon, so how many do you have left not counting Adam and the Moon Angels? 15, same as in the series not counting Adam and Lilith.

Adam 2. Lilith 3. Razie
